cards.prompt: A tree is data, not executable code; add.Compile() creates a delegate and func() invokes it, so () => 1 + 2 prints 3
cards.code: using System; using System.Linq.Expressions; Expression<Func<int>> add = () => 1 + 2; var func = add.Compile(); var answer = func(); Console.WriteLine(answer);

cards.prompt: Expression<Func<int,bool>>.Compile() returns the strongly-typed Func<int,bool>; result(4) is 4<5 True and result(9) is False, so the output is True False
cards.code: using System; using System.Linq.Expressions; Expression<Func<int,bool>> expr = num => num < 5; Func<int,bool> result = expr.Compile(); Console.WriteLine($"{result(4)} {result(9)}");

cards.prompt: An untyped LambdaExpression.Compile() returns the base Delegate; when the delegate type is not known you call DynamicInvoke, so 7*3 prints 21
cards.code: using System; using System.Linq.Expressions; var n = Expression.Parameter(typeof(int), "n"); LambdaExpression le = Expression.Lambda(Expression.Multiply(n, Expression.Constant(3)), n); Delegate d = le.Compile(); Console.WriteLine(d.DynamicInvoke(7));
